hypothesis: Incorporating a learned non-linear spectral mixing component that models inter-molecular interactions (e.g., hydrogen bonding and dipole–dipole coupling) into the mixture representation will significantly improve the ability of Transformer-based models to identify individual components from IR spectra of mixtures, especially in ternary systems and low-abundance components, and will yield higher Top-1/Top-10 accuracies on real ATR-IR data than the current linear-additivity baseline.
